title: Power Cuts & Transformer Scratches
description: Use rapid crossfader cuts in rhythm to chop audio into percussive stabs — advanced scratch technique.

steps.instruction: Watch: the crossfader snaps open-closed-open in rapid succession while the jog holds a forward position. Each snap creates a percussive cut.
Watch: the cuts happen in groups of three — cut-cut-cut on one beat. This is the transformer effect.
Watch: the cuts are in tempo with the track. Three cuts on beat one, three cuts on beat two — rhythmic and precise.

phases.instruction: The crossfader is your gate. Open it and sound comes through. Slam it shut and silence. Open-shut-open three times on one beat — that is a transformer scratch. Your wrist controls the rhythm, your ear controls the timing. Think of it as playing the crossfader like a percussive instrument.

steps.instruction: Start Deck A playing at 80% volume. Crossfader on the left (Deck A open).
Practice a single cut: snap the crossfader all the way right (closed) then all the way left (open). One clean snap.
Now do three cuts on a single beat — open-close-open-close-open-close in the time of one quarter note. Keep the hand loose.
Now add the jog wheel. Hold a sound forward with the right hand while cutting with the left. Forward sound, rapid cuts.
Try to keep the cuts in tempo with the beat. Three cuts per beat, every beat. This is advanced — be patient with yourself.
